zh.lifeSummary: 當Juliana Brown於大約1382年出生在英國時，她的父親Brown21歲，母親Bouchier24歲。她和Sir John FitzRandolph至少有3個兒子和5個女兒。她去世於1446年4月11日享年65歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭諾福爾可斯庫爾頓。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Juliana Brown于大约1382年出生在英国时，她的父亲Brown21岁，母亲Bouchier24岁。她和Sir John FitzRandolph至少有3个儿子和5个女儿。她去世于1446年4月11日享年65岁，埋葬在英国英格兰諾福爾可斯庫爾頓。

nameMeanings.name: Brown
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English, Scottish, and Irish: generally a nickname referring to the color of the hair or complexion, Middle English br(o)un, from Old English brūn or Old French brun. This word is occasionally found in Old French, Middle English and Old Norse as a personal name or byname (Middle English personal name Brun, Broun, ancient Germanic Bruno, Old English Brūn, or possibly Old Norse Brúnn or Brúni). Brun- was also an ancient Germanic name-forming element. Some instances of Old English Brūn as a personal name may therefore be short forms of compound names such as Brūngar, Brūnwine, etc. As a Scottish and Irish name, it sometimes represents a translation of Gaelic Donn (see below). Brown (including in the senses below) is the fourth most frequent surname in the US. It is also very common among African Americans and Native Americans (see also 5 below).
Irish and Scottish: adopted for Ó Duinn (see Dunn ) or for any of the many Irish and Scottish Gaelic names containing the element donn ‘brown-haired’ (also meaning ‘chieftain’), for example Donahue .
Irish: phonetic Anglicization of Mac an Bhreitheamhnaigh; see Breheny .

variants.name: Dunn
meanings: Irish: shortened Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Duinn, Ó Doinn ‘descendant of Donn’, a byname meaning ‘brown-haired’ or ‘chieftain’. Compare Dunne . This name has sometimes been Anglicized as Brown .
English: nickname for a dark complexioned or swarthy man, from Middle English dun ‘dun, dark’ (Old English dunn ‘dull brown’).
Scottish: habitational name from Dun in Angus, named with Gaelic dùn ‘fort’. Compare Dun .

variants.name: Brunson
meanings: English: usually from the Middle English personal name Brun (Old English Brūna or Old Norse Brúni) + -son ‘Brown's son’. See Brown . There may also be occasional survivals of an Old English compound personal name Brūnsunu ‘brown son’, attested in the Domesday Book, and presumably given to a child with brown hair or complexion. Whether it was also used as an adult nickname is uncertain. This form of the surname is most common in Norfolk.

variants.name: Browning
meanings: English (southern): from the Middle English personal name Brouning (Old English Brūning), a derivative of the byname Brūn (see Brown ).

History: This name was brought independently to North America from England by numerous different bearers from the 17th century onward. William Browning was one of the free planters who assented to the ‘Fundamental Agreement’ of the New Haven Colony on June 4, 1639.

variants.name: Browne
meanings: English: variant of   Brown  .
Probably also an Americanized form (translation into English) of German   Braune   or   Braun  .
Irish: phonetic Anglicization of Mac an Bhreitheamhnaigh; see Breheny .

variants.name: Burnett
meanings: Scottish and Irish (Tyrone and Antrim): descriptive nickname of Norman origin, from Old French burnete, a diminutive of brun ‘brown’ (see Brown ), or possibly in some instances a metonymic occupational name for a maker or seller of burnete, a high quality woolen cloth, originally dark brown in color.
Probably also an altered form of English   Barnett  .
In some cases also an altered form of French   Burnet  ; compare   Burnette  .

variants.name: Erjavec
meanings: Slovenian: nickname from an old spelling of rjavec, most commonly meaning ‘dark skinned man’ and ‘brown horse’. Compare Brown .

variants.name: Borron
meanings: Scottish: perhaps a variant of   Brown   in a Scottish pronunciation. This surname is very rare in Britain.

variants.name: Broun
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Russian Boris, Lyubov, Sergey.
Scottish: variant of   Brown  .
Jewish: variant of   Braun  .

nameMeanings.name: Juliana
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: feminine form of Julianus ( see Julian ), which was revived in England in the 18th century and has been used occasionally ever since.

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: When Juliana Brown was born about 1382, in England, her father, Brown, was 21 and her mother, Bouchier, was 24. She had at least 3 sons and 5 daughters with Sir John FitzRandolph. She died on 11 April 1446, at the age of 65, and was buried in Scoulton, Norfolk, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Juliana Brown est née environ 1382, à Angleterre, son père, Brown, avait 21 ans et sa mère, Bouchier, avait 24 ans. Elle a eu au moins 3 fils et 5 filles avec Sir John FitzRandolph. Elle est décédée le 11 avril 1446, à l’âge de 65 ans, et a été enterrée à Scoulton, Norfolk, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Als Juliana Brown um 1382 in England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Brown 21 und ihre Mutter Bouchier 24. Sie hatte mit Sir John FitzRandolph mindestens 3 Söhne und 5 Töchter. Sie starb am 11. April 1446 im Alter von 65 Jahren und wurde in Scoulton, Norfolk, England, United Kingdom begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Juliana Brown è nata il circa 1382 nel England, sua padre, Brown, aveva 21 anni e sua madre, Bouchier, aveva 24 anni. Lei avuto almeno 3 figli e 5 figlie con Sir John FitzRandolph. Morì il 11 aprile 1446, all'età di 65 anni, e fu sepolta a Scoulton, Norfolk, Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ja.lifeSummary: Juliana Brownが約1382年にイギリスで生まれた時，父Brownは21歳，母Bouchierは24歳であった。彼女はSir John FitzRandolphとの間に少なくとも3人の息子と5人の娘をもうけた。彼女は1446年4月11日に65歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスノーフォルクスコウルトンにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Juliana Brown(이)가 대략 1382년 영국에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Brown(은)는 21세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Bouchier(은)는 24세였다. 그녀는 Sir John FitzRandolph(와)과 적어도 세 명의 아들과 다섯 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1446년 4월 11일 65세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 노르폴크 스콜턴에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Juliana Brown nasceu aproximadamente 1382, em Inglaterra, seu pai, Brown, tinha 21 anos e sua mãe, Bouchier, tinha 24 anos. Ela teve pelo menos 3 filhos e 5 filhas com Sir John FitzRandolph. Ela faleceu em 11 de abril de 1446, com 65 anos, e foi sepultada em Scoulton, Norfolk, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Juliana Brown родилась около 1382 г. в местности Англия, её отцу по имени Brown было 21 год, а матери, которую звали Bouchier, 24. У неё было по крайней мере 3 сына и 5 дочерей от Sir John FitzRandolph. Она умерла 11 апреля 1446 г. в возрасте 65 лет и была похоронена в местности скоултон, норфолк, Англия, Великобритания.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Juliana Brown nació aproximadamente 1382, en Inglaterra, su papá, Brown, tenía 21 años y su mamá, Bouchier, tenía 24 años. Tuvo por lo menos 3 hijos y 5 hijas con Sir John FitzRandolph. Murió el 11 de abril de 1446, a la edad de 65 años, y fue sepultada en Scoulton, Norfolk, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
